series_title: General information regarding the Dept of Mental R
series_description: Information including handouts, generated by the Department, on the history of Mental Retardation, the definitions of ICF/MRs and active treatments, status of consumers in group homes and institutions.

The History of how the Medicaid Redesign came about at MRDD. Contains the Guidance package for County Boards to follow; Statutory changes; Legislation; Medicaid Service and Support Administration and Medicaid Program Management.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years after the program ends

series_description: Information that was sent to the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, the federal authority over Medicaid money, regarding the OHC Waiver for medically fragile children (disabled). Also, notes from various meetings on the OHC "2001" and the "1999" material on the Program Inefficiencies result in provider overpayments and patient risks.
Attorney General opinion on the authority of the Council of Government (COG) to hold funds; Legal Services opinion on COGs and risk fund; and discussion on implementation of the rule.

This waiver provides home and community based services to individuals, but for the provisions of such services, would require intermediate care facility for mentally retarded persons with related conditions (ICFMR) for consumers who are enrolled on the Ohio Home Care Waiver and who have an ICF-MRDD level of care. Record includes "2001" Waiver format; attachments; appendix; and definitions.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years after program ends.

series_description: Information concerning self-determination (Vision) including Vision Paper Cost Comparison; Vision Paper Fiscal Implications, Cost estimate worksheet, notes from meetings, Vision paper financial projections; system values, final reports to the Governor

The Community Access Model waiver is a Medicaid home and community based waiver that has been developed in accordance with 1915(c) of the Social Security Act. It is, in part, a response to the planned closure of two state operated developmental centers, Applec Creek and Springview. The waiver will provide residents the opportunity to live in the community. The record series includes decision memos, concept papers, administrative rules, the waiver application and related materials.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years after the end of the waiver, review by state prior to disposal.

series_description: Level 1 Waiver provides home and Community based services as authorized under the Social Security Act for individuals who absent these servcies would otherwise require care in a intermediate care facility for the mentally retarded or persons with related conditions. These records contain allocation methodology by county for the Level 1 waiver slots.
Home and Community Based Waiver Services background and outstanding issues/concerns; dental, survey of policy and correspondence.
List of agencies that are Certified Non-Governmental Habilitation Centers and county that they are from.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until updated or obsolete.

series_description: Various statistics amied at Community Alternative Funding Systems (CAFS) billing data for public schools for medicaid eligible students. Cost report comparisons of calendar year for children and schools.
Audits of POS residential cost reports and contracts. The reconciliation of POS cost reports.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years unless certified to the Attorney General''s office,retain for 2 years following their resolution.

series_description: Audits of Medicaid programs required by the federal government. (Examples: Residential Facility Waiver, Individual Options Waiver, and Community Alternative Funding System)

series_description: MRDD uses Individual Information Forms (IIF)that are submitted by the county board to develop the sample used in their review process. "IIF Information" includes demographic information specific to an individual receiving services, including date of birth, address, county of residence, family dynamics, types of county board programs/services utilized. NOTE: These units use the information that is gathered by another Department, and are not considered the primary holder of these records.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 6 years ; Electronic: Retain for 6 years

series_description: SITA compiles information from all 88 county boards regarding individuals listed on their waiting lists. Information includes the individuals name, type of service requested, and Contact information. Other units in Community Services may also use this information for review purposes, waiver determination, and other Departmental needs.
